flashcards.question: What is the mean?
flashcards.answer: The sum of all values divided by the number of values (average)

flashcards.question: What is the median?
flashcards.answer: The middle value when data is ordered from least to greatest

flashcards.question: What is the mode?
flashcards.answer: The value that appears most frequently in a data set

flashcards.question: How do you find the median of an even number of values?
Which measure of center is most affected by outliers?
When is the median preferred over the mean?
flashcards.answer: When data is skewed or has outliers

flashcards.question: What does resistant mean in statistics?
flashcards.answer: Not strongly affected by extreme values (outliers)

flashcards.question: How do you calculate the mean of {3, 5, 7}?
Can a data set have more than one mode?
flashcards.answer: Yes — it can be bimodal (2 modes) or multimodal

flashcards.question: What is a data set with no mode?
flashcards.answer: A set where no value repeats
